rationales.hedonism: She "stayed past six" and centers student outcomes over rest, treating personal comfort as secondary.
rationales.achievement: She is driven by concrete performance gains, highlighting the reading club turnout and that two students are now "a full grade level above where they started."
rationales.benevolence: She feels personally responsible when a student might fail, saying "I can't separate myself from the outcome," which shows deep care for the children she teaches.
rationales.universalism: She frames the stakes as students otherwise ending up "in the same cycle their parents were stuck in," showing concern with breaking a broader pattern of disadvantage.

rationales.hedonism: She "came in on Saturdays" and fixates on rank instead of comfort, sacrificing leisure for performance.
rationales.achievement: She is explicitly dissatisfied with being "fourth out of nine" and says only "First. Or at least second" would have felt okay, showing strong performance striving.

rationales.hedonism: She spent "two hours building supplementary inference exercises tonight instead of eating dinner," deprioritizing immediate comfort.
rationales.achievement: Rather than stopping at frustration, she analyzes the breakdown by question type and builds extra exercises to improve results.
rationales.universalism: She recognizes the test reflects "privilege" and that many students lack books or even the test's language at home, showing concern with structural inequity beyond her own standing.

rationales.self_direction: She is not waiting passively after the block office's form response; with "data" in hand, she focuses on "something I can actually push on."
rationales.universalism: She is acting on contaminated school water that "these kids drink," gathering six months of readings and considering going to the press to protect the wider community.

rationales.hedonism: On a Sunday she planned to rest but instead works through "three stacks of papers," and says the sense of missing home is "something I can't afford right now."
rationales.achievement: She evaluates herself in terms of effort and results, saying "On effort I'd say five" while still scrutinizing her performance.
rationales.universalism: One of the three urgent tasks is formatting "the water quality report" for the NGO, showing continued commitment to a public-health issue beyond her personal life.

rationales.self_direction: She sets her own limit by telling the coordinator she can do fieldwork only "every other Saturday instead of every week," despite feeling guilty about it.
rationales.universalism: She rejects "letting the NGO stuff go" because that would mean the water readings sit unused and officials keep ignoring the problem.

rationales.hedonism: She stayed up until "2 AM" rewriting materials and admits "I need to sleep more," showing clear self-denial for the work.
rationales.achievement: She reworked the module until it produced better outcomes and notes "the difference was immediate" when students used the revised passages.
rationales.universalism: She redesigns passages around "market scenes, monsoon flooding, cricket matches" because the standard materials assume a different childhood, showing concern with fairness across social and cultural backgrounds.

rationales.achievement: She tracks improvement rigorously, noting the practice pass rate rose from "38% to 61%" and documenting the evidence carefully.
rationales.universalism: She keeps pressing the water issue through multiple channels, agreeing to submit the dataset to the state environmental board and tying student performance to bias in the standardized system.

rationales.achievement: She refuses to take "three days off" because she "need[s] to prep the students for boards," prioritizing work demands and performance goals.
rationales.tradition: She is missing Pongal and staying away from home despite her family's expectations, which puts her professional commitments in conflict with an important family and cultural observance.

rationales.hedonism: She says "the water report matters more than my comfort" and "my students' futures matter more than my sleep schedule," explicitly subordinating rest and ease.
rationales.achievement: She submits an "Emerging Educator portfolio" and emphasizes measurable student gains, showing strong investment in competent performance and formal recognition.
rationales.tradition: She notes "I missed Pongal" and has not called her grandmother in weeks, showing her work is crowding out family and cultural continuity.
rationales.universalism: Her one-page note argues that performance should be judged with attention to context, and she frames the water issue and students' futures as causes bigger than herself.

rationales.security: He refuses to "just wait and see," immediately runs the numbers, and worries about needing "four extra hours a day" to protect his income against the pay change.
He abandons a routine that "works" for an untested third app, loses money on the deposit, and ends up scattered and less in control of his livelihood.
He restores the stable setup by deleting the third app, going "back to the two I know," logging earnings, and planning routine maintenance before it becomes a bigger problem.

rationales.achievement: He is motivated by the measurable target of hitting "thirty deliveries by Friday" and securing the streak bonus.
rationales.security: He knowingly keeps riding with brakes "grinding metal on metal" and repeatedly says "one more day" to avoid losing earnings, putting safety and stability at risk.
He finally repairs the brakes and notes the bike now "stops clean," explicitly restoring safe and reliable working conditions.
He refuses to put "two months of savings" into a shaky food truck idea and says plainly, "the money stays where it is," prioritizing financial protection over speculation.
rationales.benevolence: Even while deciding not to invest, he agrees to have "cafecito with him" because he doesn't want Andrés to feel dismissed, showing care toward family ties.

rationales.security: He abandons the dependable routes he "memorized" for a risky shortcut, then faces a rating hit that threatens order volume and income stability.
He transfers the bonus to savings "immediately" and tracks whether May will cover the June insurance renewal "without dipping into savings," showing deliberate protection of future stability.

He pays the bike insurance before it is due, values having "one less thing on the list," and plans the plumbing repair only once the insurance is squared away, prioritizing orderly financial and household stability.
